data.content_ref: inline:The target's mechanism formula for net benefit is dimensionally inconsistent and uses the wrong probability weight for the harm case. It states: 'The net benefit is recall_wrong × P(fragile-correct) − falseflag_rate_correct × P(fragile-correct)'. Both terms are weighted by P(fragile-correct), meaning the formula entirely ignores the probability of fragile-incorrect pools. However, the target's own mechanism text states the benefit comes from 'removes enough wrong answers to flip 3/5→2/3 (benefit)', which requires acting on fragile-INCORRECT pools. By weighting the positive recall term by P(fragile-correct) instead of P(fragile-incorrect), the formula fails to mathematically represent the mechanism it describes. The correct formula should subtract P(fragile-incorrect) from P(fragile-correct) or weight the recall term by P(fragile-incorrect). This makes the proposed strict-benefit zone prediction structurally invalid.
